question: [File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: IOracle.price / oracle reentrancy via ERC777 collateral] Can an unprivileged borrower under preconditions where the collateral token is ERC777 and the oracle reads the Midnight contract's collateral balance trigger supplyCollateral (ERC777 hook fires) -> oracle reads stale balance -> isHealthy uses stale price, violating the invariant that callbacks must not corrupt partial state through reentrancy, causing scoped impact: oracle returns inflated price during reentrancy, allowing borrowing more than the true collateral value supports? Proof idea: deploy ERC777Collateral with hook that calls oracle, assert health check uses correct post-supply balance.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: IOracle.price / oracle returning different values per call in same tx] Can an unprivileged liquidator under preconditions where a periphery oracle returns different prices on successive calls within the same transaction trigger liquidate -> bitmap loop calls oracle for each collateral -> liquidatedCollatPrice set on first pass, maxDebt computed with second-pass prices, violating the invariant that repay amount, seize amount, LIF, close factor, dust, and bad-debt realization must be mutually consistent, causing scoped impact: inconsistent oracle prices within one liquidate call make maxDebt and liquidatedCollatPrice computed at different price points, breaking RCF and seize amount calculations? Proof idea: deploy CountingOracle returning price[callCount], assert liquidate accounting is consistent.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: IOracle.price / MidnightBundles.repayAndWithdrawCollateral oracle dependency] Can an unprivileged user using MidnightBundles.repayAndWithdrawCollateral under preconditions where the oracle price drops between repay and withdrawCollateral trigger repay (succeeds, debt reduced) -> withdrawCollateral -> isHealthy (oracle returns lower price) -> UnhealthyBorrower revert, violating the invariant that valid repayments must not be frozen, causing scoped impact: user paid loan tokens to repay debt but cannot withdraw collateral because oracle moved within the bundle, effectively losing the repaid assets with collateral still locked? Proof idea: deploy DropOracle that returns lower price on second call, assert repayAndWithdrawCollateral reverts after repay.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: IOracle.price / oracle price = 1 in isHealthy] Can an unprivileged market creator under preconditions where a periphery oracle returns price = 1 (not 1e36) trigger isHealthy -> maxDebt = collateral * 1 / 1e36 * lltv / 1e18 = 0 for any reasonable collateral amount, violating the invariant that healthy accounts must not become liquidatable, causing scoped impact: all borrowers in the market are immediately liquidatable regardless of collateral amount because maxDebt rounds to 0? Proof idea: deploy OneOracle returning 1, create market, supply 1e18 collateral, borrow 1 unit, assert isHealthy returns false.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: IOracle.price / oracle price boundary at ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE] Can an unprivileged borrower under preconditions where the oracle returns exactly ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE (1e36, meaning 1:1 collateral:loan) trigger isHealthy -> maxDebt = collateral * 1e36 / 1e36 * lltv / 1e18 = collateral * lltv / 1e18, violating the invariant that every borrow/take must create correct debt and collateral obligation, causing scoped impact: verify that a position at exactly LLTV boundary (debt = collateral * lltv / 1e18) is correctly classified as healthy (maxDebt >= debt) given mulDivDown rounding? Proof idea: fuzz collateral amounts near LLTV boundary with price = 1e36, assert isHealthy matches expected health status.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: IOracle.price / oracle not called in supplyCollateral but called in liquidate] Can an unprivileged borrower under preconditions where a collateral oracle is deployed at a non-contract address (returns 0 bytes) trigger supplyCollateral (no oracle call, succeeds) -> liquidate (oracle call reverts with no code), violating the invariant that unhealthy positions must remain liquidatable, causing scoped impact: borrower activates a collateral with a non-contract oracle address, making their position permanently unliquidatable because liquidate reverts on the oracle call? Proof idea: set oracle = address(0xdead), supply collateral, borrow, assert liquidate reverts.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: IOracle.price / oracle gas griefing in bitmap loop] Can an unprivileged market creator under preconditions where a periphery oracle adapter consumes near-block-gas-limit gas per call trigger liquidate -> bitmap loop calls oracle for each of up to 16 activated collaterals, violating the invariant that unhealthy positions must remain liquidatable, causing scoped impact: gas exhaustion in the oracle loop makes liquidation of multi-collateral positions impossible, allowing bad debt to accumulate? Proof idea: deploy GasHogOracle consuming 1M gas per call, activate 16 collaterals, assert liquidate reverts with out-of-gas.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: IOracle.price / oracle price used in both maxDebt and badDebt with different rounding] Can an unprivileged liquidator under preconditions where collateral * price / ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE has a non-zero remainder trigger liquidate -> maxDebt uses mulDivDown(price, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE) while badDebt uses mulDivUp(price, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE), violating the invariant that repay amount, seize amount, LIF, close factor, dust, and bad-debt realization must be mutually consistent, causing scoped impact: rounding asymmetry between maxDebt (down) and badDebt (up) means a position can have badDebt > 0 even when maxDebt >= originalDebt, causing incorrect bad debt socialization? Proof idea: construct position where collateral*price has remainder, assert badDebt > 0 iff originalDebt > maxDebt.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: IOracle.price / MidnightBundles.buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral oracle-driven take failure] Can an unprivileged taker using MidnightBundles.buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral under preconditions where TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits computes unitsToTake = 0 due to oracle-driven settlement fee changes trigger the take loop with unitsToTake = 0 for all offers, violating the invariant that offer fill amount cannot exceed remaining offer capacity, causing scoped impact: all takes are no-ops (0 units), filledBuyerAssets stays 0, function reverts with OutOfOffers after user paid targetBuyerAssets? [File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: IOracle.price / oracle price in continuousFeeCredit adjustment during bad debt] Can an unprivileged liquidator under preconditions where a periphery oracle returns inflated price causing badDebt = 0 trigger liquidate -> continuousFeeCredit adjustment is skipped (only runs when badDebt > 0), violating the invariant that total credit, debt, collateral, fees, and token balances must stay solvent, causing scoped impact: oracle overpricing suppresses bad debt realization, leaving continuousFeeCredit unadjusted while totalUnits decreases, causing continuousFeeCredit > totalUnits accounting desync? Proof idea: create undercollateralized position, set oracle 2x true price, liquidate, assert continuousFeeCredit <= totalUnits.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: IOracle.price / oracle price in lossFactor computation] Can an unprivileged liquidator under preconditions where a periphery oracle returns price that causes badDebt = originalDebt (all debt is bad) trigger liquidate -> lossFactor = type(uint128).max - (type(uint128).max - _lossFactor).mulDivDown(_totalUnits - badDebt, _totalUnits) with _totalUnits - badDebt = 0, violating the invariant that total claimable credit must not exceed repaid loan assets, causing scoped impact: lossFactor reaches type(uint128).max, subsequent take calls revert with MarketLossFactorMaxedOut, permanently freezing the market? [File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: IOracle.price / oracle price in seizedAssets computation rounding] Can an unprivileged liquidator under preconditions where repaidUnits * lif / WAD * ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE / price has a non-zero remainder trigger liquidate(repaidUnits > 0) -> seizedAssets = repaidUnits.mulDivDown(lif, WAD).mulDivDown(ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE, price), violating the invariant that repaidUnits/seizedAssets computations round against the liquidator, causing scoped impact: mulDivDown rounding means liquidator receives slightly less collateral than the oracle price implies, creating a systematic loss for liquidators that may make liquidation unprofitable? [File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: IOracle.price / oracle price in repaidUnits computation rounding] Can an unprivileged liquidator under preconditions where seizedAssets * price / ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE * WAD / lif has a non-zero remainder trigger liquidate(seizedAssets > 0) -> repaidUnits = seizedAssets.mulDivUp(liquidatedCollatPrice, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivUp(WAD, lif), violating the invariant that repaidUnits/seizedAssets computations round against the liquidator, causing scoped impact: mulDivUp rounding means liquidator must repay slightly more than the oracle price implies for the seized collateral, creating a systematic loss? [File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: IOracle.price / oracle price in maxRepaid mulDivUp rounding] Can an unprivileged liquidator under preconditions where (debt - maxDebt) * WAD * WAD / (WAD * WAD - lif * lltv) has a non-zero remainder trigger liquidate normal mode -> maxRepaid = (debt - maxDebt).mulDivUp(WAD*WAD, WAD*WAD - lif*lltv), violating the invariant that the liquidated position could be slightly healthy after a liquidation in normal mode, causing scoped impact: maxRepaid rounds up, so after liquidation newDebt = debt - repaidUnits may be slightly below newMaxDebt, meaning the position is slightly over-collateralized post-liquidation as documented? [File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: IOracle.price / oracle price in take -> isHealthy for seller with existing debt] Can an unprivileged taker under preconditions where the seller (maker of buy offer) has existing debt and the oracle price is at the health boundary trigger take -> sellerDebtIncrease > 0 -> isHealthy check at line 476, violating the invariant that every borrow/take must create correct debt and collateral obligation, causing scoped impact: oracle price rounding in isHealthy makes the post-take position appear unhealthy (SellerIsLiquidatable revert) even though the seller's collateral should cover the new debt, blocking valid borrows? [File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: IOracle.price / oracle price in isHealthy vs liquidate maxDebt rounding asymmetry] Can an unprivileged liquidator under preconditions where isHealthy and liquidate both compute maxDebt with mulDivDown but on different code paths trigger a state where isHealthy returns true but liquidate's maxDebt check (originalDebt > maxDebt) also passes, violating the invariant that only unhealthy accounts should be liquidatable, causing scoped impact: if isHealthy and liquidate compute maxDebt identically, a position at the exact boundary (debt == maxDebt) is healthy per isHealthy but the liquidate check originalDebt > maxDebt is false, so liquidate correctly reverts - verify this boundary is consistent? [File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: IOracle.price / oracle price in MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget referral fee] Can an unprivileged taker using MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget under preconditions where referralFeePct > 0 and oracle-driven take failures cause filledSellerAssets < targetFilledSellerAssets trigger the OutOfOffers revert, violating the invariant that user funds must not be frozen, causing scoped impact: user supplied collateral (tx reverts so collateral is returned) but the referral fee formula referralFeeAssets = targetSellerAssets.mulDivDown(referralFeePct, WAD - referralFeePct) is computed on the target not the filled amount, potentially overcharging if the revert path is reached? [File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: IOracle.price / oracle price in liquidate with collateralIndex not in bitmap] Can an unprivileged liquidator under preconditions where collateralIndex is not set in the borrower's collateralBitmap trigger liquidate(seizedAssets=0, repaidUnits=0) to realize bad debt, violating the invariant that liquidation must not leave accounting desynced, causing scoped impact: liquidate with both inputs = 0 skips the seize/repay block but still computes maxDebt and badDebt using the bitmap loop, so liquidatedCollatPrice = 0 (collateralIndex not in bitmap), and the bad debt realization proceeds correctly - verify this path does not corrupt accounting? [File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: IOracle.price / oracle price in post-maturity liquidation with lif = 1] Can an unprivileged liquidator under preconditions where block.timestamp == market.maturity + 1 (lif = WAD + (maxLif - WAD) * 1 / TIME_TO_MAX_LIF ≈ WAD) trigger liquidate(postMaturityMode=true) -> seizedAssets = repaidUnits.mulDivDown(WAD, WAD).mulDivDown(ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE, price) = repaidUnits * ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE / price, violating the invariant that post-maturity liquidation incentive starts at 1, causing scoped impact: at lif = WAD the liquidator receives exactly oracle-priced collateral with no incentive, making liquidation unprofitable if gas costs exceed the spread, leaving bad debt unrealized? Proof idea: warp to maturity + 1, assert lif == WAD and liquidation is economically viable.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: IOracle.price / oracle price in liquidate with maxLif = WAD (LLTV = 1)] Can an unprivileged liquidator under preconditions where LLTV = 1e18 (LLTV_8) and maxLif = WAD trigger liquidate normal mode -> maxRepaid = lltv < WAD ? ... : type(uint256).max, violating the invariant that repay amount is capped by recovery close factor, causing scoped impact: with LLTV = 1 the RCF is deactivated (maxRepaid = type(uint256).max), so liquidator can repay all debt and seize all collateral in one call, and with lif = WAD the seizedAssets = repaidUnits * ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE / price, meaning liquidator gets exactly oracle-priced collateral with no incentive - verify this is the intended behavior? [File: 'src/interfaces/IOracle.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: IOracle.price / oracle price in liquidate with seizedAssets > collateral] Can an unprivileged liquidator under preconditions where a periphery oracle returns very low price trigger liquidate(repaidUnits > 0) -> seizedAssets = repaidUnits.mulDivDown(lif, WAD).mulDivDown(ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE, price) > position.collateral[collateralIndex], violating the invariant that collateral cannot be seized unless liquidation rules allow it, causing scoped impact: seizedAssets exceeds available collateral, causing underflow in newCollateral = position.collateral[collateralIndex] - seizedAssets (Solidity 0.8.34 reverts), permanently freezing liquidation?
